title: Principal Developer, AI Detections
company_focus: Cybersecurity managed detection and response (MDR) platform
description:
At Arctic Wolf, you won’t just watch the cybersecurity
industry evolve – you'll help lead the change. Our global
Pack is made up of people who thrive on solving hard
problems, moving fast, and building technology that protects
organizations around the world. We’re proud to be recognized
by Forbes, CNBC, Fortune, CRN, Bartner Peer Insights and IDC
MarketScape – but what matters most is the work behind it:
delivering real outcomes for customers through award winning
innovation like our Aurora Platform.

If you’re looking for meaningful work, smart teammates and
the chance to make a real impact in a high-growth company
that’s redefining security operations, Arctic Wolf is the
right place for you!

Our mission is simple: End Cyber Risk.

Role: Principal Engineer – AI Threat Intel Platform

We are seeking a Principal Engineer – AI Threat Intel
Platform to provide deep technical leadership in designing
and evolving next-generation AI-driven systems that detect
and mitigate emerging risks such as fraud, abuse, and
anomalous behavior at global scale.

This is a highly influential individual contributor role
operating at the intersection of agentic AI systems, backend
platform architecture, cloud-native scalability, and
autonomous decision-making. You will define technical
strategy, architect complex systems, and lead the design of
LangChain/LangGraph-based reasoning frameworks, scalable
backend platforms, MLOps pipelines, and autonomous AI agents
that materially enhance Arctic Wolf’s proactive defense
capabilities.

Candidates from cybersecurity, financial services, ad tech,
or other large-scale data and risk platforms are strongly
encouraged to apply.

About Arctic Wolf Labs

Arctic Wolf Labs is our research and innovation division,
dedicated to advancing AI, automation, and data intelligence
to further our mission to end cyber risk.

The Labs Engineering team works closely with AI researchers,
data scientists, security experts, and core platform teams
to invent and operationalize autonomous detection systems,
agentic reasoning frameworks, large-scale microservices
platforms, and advanced MLOps capabilities that push the
boundaries of modern cyber defense.

About the Role

- Act as a technical authority and system architect for the AI
  Threat Intel Platform, driving long-term architecture and
  design decisions.
- Lead the design and evolution of AI-driven fraud, risk, and
  anomaly detection systems operating at scale.
- Architect and build advanced agentic AI frameworks using
  LangChain, LangGraph, or similar technologies for
  reasoning, orchestration, and autonomous execution.
- Design and guide implementation of highly scalable backend
  platforms and microservices using Python, Go, and cloud-
  native technologies.
- Own architectural patterns for resilience, performance,
  observability, and security across distributed systems.
- Design and influence event-driven, real-time pipelines using
  AWS services such as RDS, DynamoDB, Lambda,
  EKS/Kubernetes, streaming, and messaging frameworks.
- Define and evolve MLOps architecture, including model
  lifecycle management, deployment strategies, monitoring,
  and real-time inference at scale.
- Drive integration of LLMs and autonomous agents for dynamic
  pattern detection, correlation, decision automation, and
  adaptive response.
- Set engineering standards for code quality, testing,
  documentation, and operational excellence across teams.
- Partner with product, data science, threat intelligence, and
  platform leadership to align technical execution with
  business and security outcomes.
- Lead complex technical initiatives across multiple teams
  without direct authority, influencing through expertise
  and collaboration.
- Mentor senior and junior engineers, grow technical talent,
  and raise the overall engineering bar.
- Contribute to R&D initiatives, technical reviews,
  architecture forums, demos, and innovation programs.

About You

- 15+ years of experience in software engineering,
  backend/platform development, distributed systems, or
  applied machine learning.
- Proven track record as a Senior Staff / Principal-level
  engineer driving architecture and delivering complex,
  high-impact systems.
- Deep expertise in backend and distributed systems, including
  large-scale, real-time, or data-intensive platforms.
- Strong experience with fraud detection, risk analytics,
  behavioral modeling, or anomaly detection in domains such
  as cybersecurity, fintech, or ad tech.
- Hands-on experience designing or integrating agentic AI
  systems (LangChain, LangGraph, Semantic Kernel, or
  similar).
- Expert-level programming skills in Python (preferred) and/or
  Go.
- Strong understanding of ML frameworks such as PyTorch,
  TensorFlow, or Scikit-learn, with practical production
  experience.
- Extensive experience with cloud-native architectures,
  Kubernetes, Docker, Terraform, and event-driven systems.
- Deep knowledge of MLOps, data pipelines, feature
  engineering, model deployment, and observability.
- Strong grasp of LLM orchestration, prompt strategies,
  evaluation, and AI system reliability.
- Experience operating and scaling systems on AWS or
  equivalent cloud platforms.
- Demonstrated ability to lead ambiguous, high-impact
  initiatives independently.
- Excellent communication skills with the ability to influence
  senior technical and non-technical stakeholders.
- Passion for mentoring, technical excellence, and building
  durable, future-proof platforms.

Don’t meet all the requirements? That’s okay! We still want
you to apply. We have many opportunities, and we are always
looking for top talent.

On-Camera Policy

To support a fair, transparent, and engaging interview
experience, candidates interviewing remotely are expected to
be on camera during all video interviews. Being on camera
fosters authentic connection, improves communication, and
allows for full engagement from both candidates and
interviewers. We understand that technical, bandwidth, or
location-related challenges may occasionally prevent video
use. If this applies, candidates are required to notify us
in advance so we can explore appropriate accommodations.

At Arctic Wolf, we foster a collaborative and inclusive work
environment that thrives on diversity of thought,
background, and culture. This is reflected in our multiple
awards, including Top Workplace USA (2021-2025), Best Places
to Work – USA (2021-2025), Great Place to Work – Canada
(2021-2024), Great Place to Work – UK (2024-2026), and
Kununu Top Company – Germany (2024-2026). Our commitment to
bold growth and shaping the future of security operations is
matched by our dedication to customer satisfaction, with
over 10,000 customers worldwide and more than 2,000 channel
partners globally. As we continue to expand globally and
enhance our technology, Arctic Wolf remains the most trusted
name in the industry.

Our Values

Arctic Wolf recognizes that success comes from delighting
our customers, so we work together to ensure that happens
every day. We believe in diversity and inclusion, and truly
value the unique qualities and unique perspectives all
employees bring to the organization. And we appreciate
that—by protecting people’s and organizations’ sensitive
data and seeking to end cyber risk— we get to work in an
industry that is fundamental to the greater good.

We celebrate unique perspectives by creating a platform for
all voices to be heard through our Pack Unity program. We
encourage all employees to join or create a new alliance.
See more about our Pack Unity here.

We also believe and practice corporate responsibility, and
have recently joined the Pledge 1% Movement, ensuring that
we continue to give back to our community. We know that
through our mission to End Cyber Risk we will continue to
engage and give back to our communities.

All wolves receive compelling compensation and benefits
packages, including:

- Equity for all employees
- Flexible annual leave, paid holidays and volunteer days
- Training and career development programs
- Comprehensive private benefits plan including medical
  insurance for you and your family, life insurance (3x
  compensation), and personal accident insurance.
- Fertility support and paid parental leave

Arctic Wolf is an Equal Opportunity Employer and considers
applicants for employment without regard to race, color,
religion, sex, orientation, national origin, age,
disability, genetics, or any other basis forbidden under
federal, provincial, or local law. Arctic Wolf is committed
to fostering a welcoming, accessible, respectful, and
inclusive environment ensuring equal access and
participation for people with disabilities. As such, we
strive to make our entire experience as accessible as
possible and provide accommodations as required for
candidates and employees with disabilities and/or other
specific needs where possible. Please let us know if you
require any accommodation by emailing
recruiting@arcticwolf.com.   View our Hiring Page to learn
more about our application process.

Security Requirements

- Conducts duties and responsibilities in accordance with
  AWN’s Information Security policies, standards, processes,
  and controls to protect the confidentiality, integrity and
  availability of AWN business information (in accordance
  with our employee handbook and corporate policies).
- Background checks are required for this position.
- This position may require access to information protected
  under U.S. export control laws and regulations, including
  the Export Administration Regulations (“EAR”).  Please
  note that, if applicable, an offer for employment will be
  conditioned on authorization to receive software or
  technology controlled under these U.S. export control laws
  and regulations.

Agentic AI reasoning frameworks for threat intelligence analysis

ai_classification_reasoning:
This role is squarely focused on building and architecting
AI-driven systems. The engineer will design
LangChain/LangGraph-based agentic AI frameworks, integrate
LLMs for autonomous decision-making, define MLOps
architecture for model lifecycle and deployment, and build
AI-powered threat detection systems. This is hands-on work
with AI/ML models, agents, and GenAI frameworks.
